"""
Social features: Friends, Messages, Challenges, Guilds
"""

from app import db
from models.user import User
from models.social import Friend, Message, Challenge, Guild, GuildMember
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

class SocialService:
    """Manage social interactions"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def send_friend_request(from_user_id: int, to_user_id: int) -> Dict:
        """Send friend request"""
        
        # Check if already friends
        existing = Friend.query.filter(
            ((Friend.user_id_1 == from_user_id) & (Friend.user_id_2 == to_user_id)) |
            ((Friend.user_id_1 == to_user_id) & (Friend.user_id_2 == from_user_id))
        ).first()
        
        if existing:
            return {'error': 'Already friends or request pending'}
        
        friend_request = Friend(
            user_id_1=from_user_id,
            user_id_2=to_user_id,
            status='pending'
        )
        
        db.session.add(friend_request)
        db.session.commit()
        
        return {
            'message': 'Friend request sent',
            'request_id': friend_request.id,
            'status': 'pending'
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def accept_friend_request(friend_request_id: int) -> Dict:
        """Accept friend request"""
        
        friend_req = Friend.query.get(friend_request_id)
        
        if not friend_req:
            return {'error': 'Request not found'}
        
        friend_req.status = 'accepted'
        friend_req.accepted_at = datetime.utcnow()
        
        db.session.commit()
        
        return {
            'message': 'Friend request accepted',
            'friendship_id': friend_req.id
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def get_friends(user_id: int) -> List[Dict]:
        """Get user's friends"""
        
        friends = Friend.query.filter(
            ((Friend.user_id_1 == user_id) | (Friend.user_id_2 == user_id)) &
            (Friend.status == 'accepted')
        ).all()
        
        friend_list = []
        for f in friends:
            friend_id = f.user_id_2 if f.user_id_1 == user_id else f.user_id_1
            friend_user = User.query.get(friend_id)
            
            friend_list.append({
                'id': friend_user.id,
                'username': friend_user.username,
                'xp': friend_user.total_xp,
                'level': friend_user.current_level,
                'connected_at': f.accepted_at.isoformat() if f.accepted_at else None
            })
        
        return friend_list
    
    @staticmethod
    def send_message(from_user_id: int, to_user_id: int, content: str) -> Dict:
        """Send direct message"""
        
        message = Message(
            from_user_id=from_user_id,
            to_user_id=to_user_id,
            content=content,
            is_read=False
        )
        
        db.session.add(message)
        db.session.commit()
        
        return {
            'message_id': message.id,
            'created_at': message.created_at.isoformat()
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def get_messages(user_id: int, other_user_id: int, limit: int = 50) -> List[Dict]:
        """Get conversation history"""
        
        messages = Message.query.filter(
            ((Message.from_user_id == user_id) & (Message.to_user_id == other_user_id)) |
            ((Message.from_user_id == other_user_id) & (Message.to_user_id == user_id))
        ).order_by(Message.created_at.desc()).limit(limit).all()
        
        # Mark as read
        for msg in messages:
            if msg.to_user_id == user_id and not msg.is_read:
                msg.is_read = True
        
        db.session.commit()
        
        return [{
            'id': msg.id,
            'from_user_id': msg.from_user_id,
            'to_user_id': msg.to_user_id,
            'content': msg.content,
            'is_read': msg.is_read,
            'created_at': msg.created_at.isoformat()
        } for msg in reversed(messages)]
    
    @staticmethod
    def create_challenge(challenger_id: int, opponent_id: int, challenge_type: str) -> Dict:
        """Create competitive challenge"""
        
        challenge = Challenge(
            challenger_id=challenger_id,
            opponent_id=opponent_id,
            challenge_type=challenge_type,  # 'speed_race', 'accuracy_battle', 'xp_race'
            status='waiting'
        )
        
        db.session.add(challenge)
        db.session.commit()
        
        return {
            'challenge_id': challenge.id,
            'type': challenge_type,
            'status': 'waiting_for_opponent'
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def submit_challenge_response(challenge_id: int, user_id: int, response_time: float, accuracy: float) -> Dict:
        """Submit challenge response"""
        
        challenge = Challenge.query.get(challenge_id)
        
        if not challenge:
            return {'error': 'Challenge not found'}
        
        if challenge.challenger_id == user_id:
            challenge.challenger_response_time = response_time
            challenge.challenger_accuracy = accuracy
        else:
            challenge.opponent_response_time = response_time
            challenge.opponent_accuracy = accuracy
        
        # If both submitted, determine winner
        if challenge.challenger_response_time and challenge.opponent_response_time:
            challenger_score = (challenge.challenger_accuracy * 100) + (1000 / challenge.challenger_response_time)
            opponent_score = (challenge.opponent_accuracy * 100) + (1000 / challenge.opponent_response_time)
            
            challenge.winner_id = challenge.challenger_id if challenger_score > opponent_score else challenge.opponent_id
            challenge.status = 'completed'
            
            # Award XP to winner
            winner = User.query.get(challenge.winner_id)
            winner.total_xp += 50
            
            db.session.add(challenge)
            db.session.commit()
            
            return {
                'challenge_id': challenge.id,
                'status': 'completed',
                'winner_id': challenge.winner_id,
                'xp_awarded': 50
            }
        
        db.session.commit()
        
        return {
            'challenge_id': challenge.id,
            'status': 'waiting_for_opponent'
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def create_guild(creator_id: int, name: str, description: str) -> Dict:
        """Create a guild/team"""
        
        guild = Guild(
            name=name,
            description=description,
            creator_id=creator_id,
            member_count=1
        )
        
        db.session.add(guild)
        db.session.flush()
        
        # Add creator as member
        member = GuildMember(
            guild_id=guild.id,
            user_id=creator_id,
            role='owner'
        )
        
        db.session.add(member)
        db.session.commit()
        
        return {
            'guild_id': guild.id,
            'name': guild.name,
            'created_at': guild.created_at.isoformat()
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def join_guild(guild_id: int, user_id: int) -> Dict:
        """Join guild"""
        
        guild = Guild.query.get(guild_id)
        
        if not guild:
            return {'error': 'Guild not found'}
        
        existing = GuildMember.query.filter_by(
            guild_id=guild_id,
            user_id=user_id
        ).first()
        
        if existing:
            return {'error': 'Already a member'}
        
        member = GuildMember(
            guild_id=guild_id,
            user_id=user_id,
            role='member'
        )
        
        guild.member_count += 1
        
        db.session.add(member)
        db.session.commit()
        
        return {
            'message': 'Joined guild',
            'guild_id': guild_id,
            'member_count': guild.member_count
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def get_guild_stats(guild_id: int) -> Dict:
        """Get guild statistics"""
        
        guild = Guild.query.get(guild_id)
        
        if not guild:
            return {'error': 'Guild not found'}
        
        members = GuildMember.query.filter_by(guild_id=guild_id).all()
        member_users = [User.query.get(m.user_id) for m in members]
        
        total_guild_xp = sum([u.total_xp for u in member_users])
        avg_level = sum([u.current_level for u in member_users]) / len(member_users) if member_users else 0
        
        return {
            'guild_id': guild.id,
            'name': guild.name,
            'members': len(member_users),
            'total_xp': total_guild_xp,
            'average_level': round(avg_level, 2),
            'created_at': guild.created_at.isoformat(),
            'member_list': [
                {
                    'username': u.username,
                    'xp': u.total_xp,
                    'level': u.current_level,
                    'role': next(m.role for m in members if m.user_id == u.id)
                }
                for u in member_users
            ]
        }

"""

import numpy as np
from typing import List, Dict, Tuple
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
from models.user import User
from models.activity import ActivityProgress, Activity
from models.progress import StudentProgress
import json

class AIRecommendationEngine:
    """Intelligent activity and friend recommendations"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.activity_dimension = 5  # Self-Awareness, Communication, Decision, Creativity, Wellness
    
    def recommend_activities(self, user_id: int, top_n: int = 5) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Recommend activities based on:
        - User's weak areas
        - Performance history
        - Learning style
        - Age appropriateness
        """
        
        user = User.query.get(user_id)
        progress = StudentProgress.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id).first()
        
        if not progress:
            return []
        
        # Get user's XP profile
        xp_profile = self._get_xp_profile(progress)
        
        # Identify weak areas (lowest XP)
        weak_areas = self._identify_weak_areas(xp_profile)
        
        # Get activities in weak areas
        recommendations = []
        
        for area_idx, weakness_score in weak_areas[:3]:  # Top 3 weak areas
            level_map = {
                0: 1,  # Self-Awareness
                1: 2,  # Communication
                2: 3,  # Decision Making
                3: 4,  # Creativity
                4: 5   # Wellness
            }
            
            level = level_map.get(area_idx, 1)
            
            # Get activities for this level
            activities = Activity.query.filter_by(
                level=level,
                is_active=True
            ).all()
            
            # Filter based on age
            age_appropriate = [
                a for a in activities 
                if self._is_age_appropriate(a, user.age)
            ]
            
            # Score activities
            for activity in age_appropriate:
                # Check if already completed
                existing = ActivityProgress.query.filter_by(
                    user_id=user_id,
                    activity_id=activity.id
                ).first()
                
                if not existing:
                    score = self._calculate_activity_score(activity, user, weakness_score)
                    recommendations.append({
                        'activity_id': activity.id,
                        'title': activity.title,
                        'level': activity.level,
                        'xp_reward': activity.xp_reward,
                        'reason': f'Strengthen your {self._get_skill_name(area_idx)}',
                        'relevance_score': score,
                        'estimated_time': activity.duration_minutes
                    })
        
        # Sort by relevance and return top N
        recommendations.sort(key=lambda x: x['relevance_score'], reverse=True)
        return recommendations[:top_n]
    
    def recommend_friends(self, user_id: int, top_n: int = 5) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Recommend friends based on:
        - Similar skill levels
        - Shared interests
        - Geographic proximity (if available)
        - Complementary strengths
        """
        
        user = User.query.get(user_id)
        all_users = User.query.filter(User.id != user_id, User.is_active == True).all()
        
        # Get current friends to exclude
        from models.social import Friend
        friends = Friend.query.filter(
            ((Friend.user_id_1 == user_id) | (Friend.user_id_2 == user_id)) &
            (Friend.status == 'accepted')
        ).all()
        
        friend_ids = set()
        for f in friends:
            friend_ids.add(f.user_id_1 if f.user_id_2 == user_id else f.user_id_2)
        
        recommendations = []
        
        for potential_friend in all_users:
            if potential_friend.id in friend_ids:
                continue
            
            # Calculate compatibility score
            compatibility = self._calculate_friend_compatibility(user, potential_friend)
            
            recommendations.append({
                'user_id': potential_friend.id,
                'username': potential_friend.username,
                'age': potential_friend.age,
                'level': potential_friend.current_level,
                'xp': potential_friend.total_xp,
                'compatibility_score': compatibility,
                'age_difference': abs(user.age - potential_friend.age),
                'level_difference': abs(user.current_level - potential_friend.current_level)
            })
        
        # Sort and return top N
        recommendations.sort(key=lambda x: x['compatibility_score'], reverse=True)
        return recommendations[:top_n]
    
    def get_learning_insights(self, user_id: int) -> Dict:
        """
        Generate personalized learning insights
        """
        
        user = User.query.get(user_id)
        progress = StudentProgress.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id).first()
        
        if not progress:
            return {}
        
        # Get recent activities
        recent_activities = ActivityProgress.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id).order_by(
            ActivityProgress.completed_at.desc()
        ).limit(20).all()
        
        if not recent_activities:
            return {'message': 'No activities completed yet'}
        
        # Calculate metrics
        avg_accuracy = np.mean([a.accuracy for a in recent_activities])
        avg_score = np.mean([a.score for a in recent_activities])
        avg_time = np.mean([a.time_spent_seconds for a in recent_activities])
        
        improvement_trend = self._calculate_improvement_trend(recent_activities)
        learning_pace = self._analyze_learning_pace(recent_activities)
        
        xp_profile = self._get_xp_profile(progress)
        strengths = self._identify_strong_areas(xp_profile)
        weaknesses = self._identify_weak_areas(xp_profile)
        
        return {
            'metrics': {
                'average_accuracy': round(avg_accuracy, 2),
                'average_score': round(avg_score, 2),
                'average_time_minutes': round(avg_time / 60, 2),
                'recent_activities_count': len(recent_activities)
            },
            'trends': {
                'improvement': improvement_trend,
                'learning_pace': learning_pace,
                'velocity': self._calculate_velocity(recent_activities)
            },
            'strengths': [
                {
                    'skill': self._get_skill_name(idx),
                    'xp': xp,
                    'proficiency': self._calculate_proficiency(xp)
                }
                for idx, xp in strengths
            ],
            'areas_for_improvement': [
                {
                    'skill': self._get_skill_name(idx),
                    'xp': xp,
                    'suggested_focus': f'Dedicate 10-15 minutes daily to {self._get_skill_name(idx)} activities'
                }
                for idx, xp in weaknesses
            ],
            'personalized_advice': self._generate_personalized_advice(user, recent_activities, xp_profile),
            'next_milestone': self._calculate_next_milestone(user),
            'daily_goal': self._suggest_daily_goal(user, learning_pace)
        }
    
    def predict_user_performance(self, user_id: int, activity_id: int) -> Dict:
        """
        Predict how user will perform on a specific activity
        """
        
        user = User.query.get(user_id)
        activity = Activity.query.get(activity_id)
        
        if not activity:
            return {'error': 'Activity not found'}
        
        # Get similar activities user completed
        similar_activities = Activity.query.filter_by(
            level=activity.level,
            activity_type=activity.activity_type
        ).all()
        
        similar_performances = []
        for similar in similar_activities:
            perf = ActivityProgress.query.filter_by(
                user_id=user_id,
                activity_id=similar.id
            ).first()
            
            if perf:
                similar_performances.append(perf)
        
        if not similar_performances:
            # Default prediction for new activity type
            return {
                'predicted_accuracy': 65,
                'predicted_score': 70,
                'estimated_time_minutes': activity.duration_minutes,
                'difficulty_match': 'medium',
                'confidence': 'low'
            }
        
        # Calculate average performance on similar activities
        avg_accuracy = np.mean([p.accuracy for p in similar_performances])
        avg_score = np.mean([p.score for p in similar_performances])
        avg_time = np.mean([p.time_spent_seconds for p in similar_performances])
        
        # Adjust based on activity difficulty
        difficulty_adjustment = self._get_difficulty_adjustment(activity, user)
        
        return {
            'predicted_accuracy': round(avg_accuracy * difficulty_adjustment, 1),
            'predicted_score': round(avg_score * difficulty_adjustment, 1),
            'estimated_time_minutes': round(avg_time / 60, 1),
            'difficulty_match': self._match_difficulty_to_user(activity, user),
            'confidence': 'high' if len(similar_performances) > 3 else 'medium'
        }
    
    # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Helper Methods
    # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    def _get_xp_profile(self, progress: StudentProgress) -> np.ndarray:
        """Convert XP data to feature vector"""
        return np.array([
            progress.level1_awareness_xp,
            progress.level2_communication_xp,
            progress.level3_decision_xp,
            progress.level4_creativity_xp,
            progress.level5_wellness_xp
        ])
    
    def _identify_weak_areas(self, xp_profile: np.ndarray) -> List[Tuple[int, float]]:
        """Find areas needing improvement"""
        
        # Normalize and find weakest
        max_xp = np.max(xp_profile)
        normalized = 1 - (xp_profile / (max_xp + 1))
        
        weak_indices = np.argsort(-normalized)  # Sort descending
        
        return [(int(idx), float(normalized[idx])) for idx in weak_indices]
    
    def _identify_strong_areas(self, xp_profile: np.ndarray) -> List[Tuple[int, float]]:
        """Find areas of strength"""
        
        strong_indices = np.argsort(-xp_profile)  # Sort descending
        
        return [(int(idx), float(xp_profile[idx])) for idx in strong_indices[:2]]
    
    def _calculate_friend_compatibility(self, user1: User, user2: User) -> float:
        """Calculate compatibility score between two users"""
        
        # Age similarity (0-1)
        age_diff = abs(user1.age - user2.age)
        age_score = max(0, 1 - (age_diff / 10))
        
        # Level similarity (0-1)
        level_diff = abs(user1.current_level - user2.current_level)
        level_score = max(0, 1 - (level_diff / 8))
        
        # Activity variety check
        user1_progress = StudentProgress.query.filter_by(user_id=user1.id).first()
        user2_progress = StudentProgress.query.filter_by(user_id=user2.id).first()
        
        if user1_progress and user2_progress:
            profile1 = self._get_xp_profile(user1_progress)
            profile2 = self._get_xp_profile(user2_progress)
            
            # Cosine similarity
            if np.linalg.norm(profile1) > 0 and np.linalg.norm(profile2) > 0:
                similarity = cosine_similarity(
                    profile1.reshape(1, -1),
                    profile2.reshape(1, -1)
                )[0][0]
            else:
                similarity = 0.5
        else:
            similarity = 0.5
        
        # Weighted average
        compatibility = (age_score * 0.3) + (level_score * 0.3) + (similarity * 0.4)
        
        return round(compatibility, 2)
    
    def _is_age_appropriate(self, activity: Activity, user_age: int) -> bool:
        """Check if activity is age-appropriate"""
        
        if user_age <= 9:
            return activity.level <= 2
        elif user_age <= 13:
            return activity.level <= 4
        else:
            return True
    
    def _calculate_activity_score(self, activity: Activity, user: User, weakness_score: float) -> float:
        """Score activity for relevance"""
        
        # Higher weakness score = higher priority
        priority_score = weakness_score * 0.5
        
        # XP reward factor
        xp_score = min(1.0, activity.xp_reward / 50) * 0.3
        
        # Duration appropriateness
        duration_score = max(0, 1 - abs(activity.duration_minutes - 5) / 10) * 0.2
        
        return round(priority_score + xp_score + duration_score, 2)
    
    def _calculate_improvement_trend(self, activities: List[ActivityProgress]) -> str:
        """Analyze performance trend"""
        
        if len(activities) < 2:
            return "insufficient_data"
        
        recent = [a.score for a in activities[:5]]
        older = [a.score for a in activities[5:10]] if len(activities) > 5 else recent
        
        avg_recent = np.mean(recent)
        avg_older = np.mean(older)
        
        improvement = ((avg_recent - avg_older) / (avg_older + 1)) * 100
        
        if improvement > 10:
            return "improving_rapidly"
        elif improvement > 0:
            return "improving_steadily"
        elif improvement > -10:
            return "stable"
        else:
            return "needs_attention"
    
    def _analyze_learning_pace(self, activities: List[ActivityProgress]) -> str:
        """Analyze learning speed"""
        
        if len(activities) < 3:
            return "just_started"
        
        times = [a.time_spent_seconds for a in activities[-5:]]
        avg_time = np.mean(times)
        
        if avg_time < 120:  # 2 minutes
            return "fast_learner"
        elif avg_time < 300:  # 5 minutes
            return "steady_learner"
        else:
            return "careful_learner"
    
    def _calculate_velocity(self, activities: List[ActivityProgress]) -> str:
        """Calculate current learning velocity"""
        
        recent_xp = sum([a.xp_earned for a in activities[:5]])
        
        if recent_xp > 75:
            return "🔥 Accelerating!"
        elif recent_xp > 40:
            return "📈 Steady progress"
        else:
            return "🐢 Building momentum"
    
    def _generate_personalized_advice(self, user: User, activities: List[ActivityProgress], xp_profile: np.ndarray) -> List[str]:
        """Generate actionable advice"""
        
        advice = []
        
        # Age-based advice
        if user.age <= 9:
            advice.append("🎨 Try drawing activities - they help express feelings!")
            advice.append("🎮 Take movement breaks between activities")
        elif user.age <= 13:
            advice.append("💭 Reflect on 'why' behind your choices")
            advice.append("👥 Complete communication activities with friends")
        else:
            advice.append("📊 Use analytics to track your progress patterns")
            advice.append("🎯 Set specific goals for each skill level")
        
        # Performance-based advice
        if np.min(xp_profile) < 20:
            advice.append("⚡ Focus on your weakest skill - even 5 mins daily helps!")
        
        if len(activities) < 5:
            advice.append("🚀 Complete more activities to unlock advanced features")
        else:
            advice.append("🏆 Try challenging yourself with different activity types")
        
        return advice
    
    def _calculate_next_milestone(self, user: User) -> Dict:
        """Calculate next achievement milestone"""
        
        milestones = [
            (50, "🚀 First 50 XP"),
            (100, "💯 Century Club"),
            (250, "🎯 Quarter K"),
            (500, "🌟 Half K"),
            (1000, "👑 Legendary"),
        ]
        
        for xp_threshold, milestone_name in milestones:
            if user.total_xp < xp_threshold:
                xp_needed = xp_threshold - user.total_xp
                return {
                    'milestone': milestone_name,
                    'xp_needed': xp_needed,
                    'progress': round((user.total_xp / xp_threshold) * 100, 1)
                }
        
        return {
            'milestone': '🌌 Beyond Legendary',
            'xp_needed': 0,
            'progress': 100
        }
    
    def _suggest_daily_goal(self, user: User, learning_pace: str) -> int:
        """Suggest daily XP goal"""
        
        if learning_pace == "fast_learner":
            return 50
        elif learning_pace == "steady_learner":
            return 30
        else:
            return 15
    
    def _get_skill_name(self, idx: int) -> str:
        """Get skill name from index"""
        
        skills = [
            "Self-Awareness",
            "Communication",
            "Decision-Making",
            "Creativity",
            "Wellness"
        ]
        
        return skills[idx] if idx < len(skills) else "Unknown"
    
    def _calculate_proficiency(self, xp: float) -> str:
        """Convert XP to proficiency level"""
        
        if xp < 30:
            return "Beginner"
        elif xp < 80:
            return "Intermediate"
        elif xp < 150:
            return "Advanced"
        else:
            return "Expert"
    
    def _get_difficulty_adjustment(self, activity: Activity, user: User) -> float:
        """Adjust prediction based on user-activity match"""
        
        progress = StudentProgress.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).first()
        
        if not progress:
            return 0.8
        
        xp_profile = self._get_xp_profile(progress)
        level_xp = xp_profile[activity.level - 1] if activity.level <= 5 else xp_profile[-1]
        
        if level_xp < 20:
            return 0.7  # Might struggle
        elif level_xp > 100:
            return 1.1  # Likely to excel
        else:
            return 0.9  # About right
    
    def _match_difficulty_to_user(self, activity: Activity, user: User) -> str:
        """Rate difficulty match"""
        
        progress = StudentProgress.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).first()
        
        if not progress:
            return "suitable"
        
        xp_profile = self._get_xp_profile(progress)
        level_xp = xp_profile[activity.level - 1] if activity.level <= 5 else xp_profile[-1]
        
        if level_xp < 15:
            return "challenging"
        elif level_xp > 120:
            return "easy"
        else:
            return "just_right"

"""

from app import db
from datetime import datetime

class Friend(db.Model):
    """Friend connections"""
    __tablename__ = 'friends'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id_1 = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    user_id_2 = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    
    status = db.Column(db.String(20), default='pending')  # pending, accepted, blocked
    
    requested_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    accepted_at = db.Column(db.DateTime)
    
    def __repr__(self):
        return f'<Friend {self.user_id_1} <-> {self.user_id_2}>'


class Message(db.Model):
    """Direct messages"""
    __tablename__ = 'messages'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    from_user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    to_user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    
    content = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
    is_read = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=False)
    
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)


class Challenge(db.Model):
    """Competitive challenges"""
    __tablename__ = 'challenges'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    challenger_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    opponent_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    
    challenge_type = db.Column(db.String(50))  # speed_race, accuracy_battle, xp_race
    status = db.Column(db.String(20), default='waiting')  # waiting, completed, cancelled
    
    challenger_response_time = db.Column(db.Float)
    challenger_accuracy = db.Column(db.Float)
    
    opponent_response_time = db.Column(db.Float)
    opponent_accuracy = db.Column(db.Float)
    
    winner_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'))
    
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    completed_at = db.Column(db.DateTime)


class Guild(db.Model):
    """Team/Guild"""
    __tablename__ = 'guilds'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = db.Column(db.Text)
    creator_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    
    member_count = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    total_guild_xp = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    
    def __repr__(self):
        return f'<Guild {self.name}>'


class GuildMember(db.Model):
    """Guild membership"""
    __tablename__ = 'guild_members'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    guild_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('guilds.id'), nullable=False)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    
    role = db.Column(db.String(20), default='member')  # owner, moderator, member
    joined_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

"""
Social interaction routes
"""

from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify
from flask_jwt_extended import jwt_required, get_jwt_identity
from services.social_service import SocialService
from services.ai_recommendation_engine import AIRecommendationEngine

social_bp = Blueprint('social', __name__)

@social_bp.route('/friends/request', methods=['POST'])
@jwt_required()
def send_friend_request():
    """Send friend request"""
    user_id = get_jwt_identity()
    data = request.get_json()
    
    result = SocialService.send_friend_request(user_id, data.get('to_user_id'))
    
    return jsonify(result), 201 if 'error' not in result else 400

@social_bp.route('/friends/accept/<int:friend_request_id>', methods=['POST'])
@jwt_required()
def accept_friend_request(friend_request_id):
    """Accept friend request"""
    result = SocialService.accept_friend_request(friend_request_id)
    
    return jsonify(result), 200 if 'error' not in result else 400

@social_bp.route('/friends', methods=['GET'])
@jwt_required()
def get_friends():
    """Get user's friends"""
    user_id = get_jwt_identity()
    friends = SocialService.get_friends(user_id)
    
    return jsonify({'friends': friends}), 200

@social_bp.route('/messages/<int:other_user_id>', methods=['GET'])

    """Get messages with user"""
    user_id = get_jwt_identity()
    limit = request.args.get('limit', 50, type=int)

@social_bp.route('/insights', methods=['GET'])

# Repository Code Search Guide

This ability provides comprehensive guidance for searching and navigating code in GitHub repositories. You have two powerful search tools available: **semantic-code-search** and **lexical-code-search**. Understanding when and how to use each is critical for effective code exploration.

## Available Search Tools

### semantic-code-search
**What it does:** Understands the meaning and context of your query to find conceptually relevant code.

**When to use:**
- User asks conceptual questions: "How does X work?", "Where is Y implemented?"
- Exploring unfamiliar codebases to understand architecture or features
- Finding code related to a specific functionality or concept
- No specific symbol names or file paths are known
- Need to understand patterns or approaches used in the codebase

**Key characteristics:**
- Query MUST be a complete sentence describing what you're looking for
- Returns code snippets ranked by semantic relevance
- Repository-scoped only (one repo at a time)

**Examples:**
| User question | Tool call |
|--------------|-----------|
| "How does authentication work in owner/repo?" | semantic-code-search: query="How does authentication work?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="repo" |
| "Where is rate limiting implemented?" | semantic-code-search: query="Where is rate limiting implemented?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="repo" |
| "How are database errors handled?" | semantic-code-search: query="How are database connection errors handled?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="repo" |

### lexical-code-search
**What it does:** Performs exact pattern matching using qualifiers to find specific code elements.

**When to use:**
- User mentions specific symbols, functions, or class names
- Looking for EXACT text matches (error messages, specific strings)
- Need to find files by path or name patterns
- Searching across multiple repositories or organizations
- User provides EXACT code snippets to find
- Following up on semantic search to find exact definitions

**Key qualifiers:**
| Qualifier | Purpose | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| symbol: | Find function/class/method definitions | symbol:AuthService |
| content: | Match exact text in files | content:"error code 500" |
| path: | Match file paths (use regex) | path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*helper[^\\/]*$/ |
| repo: | Scope to specific repository | repo:owner/name |
| org: | Scope to organization | org:github |
| language: | Filter by programming language | language:python |
| is: | Filter by properties | is:archived, is:fork, is:vendored, is:generated |

**Symbol construction:**

Use symbol: when users ask about function, class, or method definitions.

Common patterns:
| User asks | Interpretation | Tool call |
|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| "Where is class Helper defined in monalisa/net?" | Looking for class definition | query="symbol:Helper", scopingQuery="repo:monalisa/net" |
| "What functions are in Foo.go?" | Looking for symbols in file | query="symbol:Foo" |
| "Describe the method MyFunc" | Looking for method definition | query="symbol:MyFunc" |
| "Find AuthService class" | Looking for class definition | query="symbol:AuthService" |
| "Where is authenticate function?" | Looking for function definition | query="symbol:authenticate" |

**Boolean operators:** Use OR, NOT, AND to combine conditions
- Example: symbol:authenticate NOT is:archived

**Parameters:**
- query: The search query with qualifiers
- scopingQuery: (optional) Additional scoping like repo:, org:, path:, language:

**Constructing path queries:**

When users ask for files in specific directories or with certain names, construct regex paths.

Common patterns:
- Any file with "helper" anywhere: path:/.*helper[^\\/]*$/
- Specific directory, any file: path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*$/
- File with extension: path:/.*\\.test\\.js$/

**Examples:**
| User question | Tool call |
|--------------|-----------|
| "Find the PaymentService class definition" | lexical-code-search: query="symbol:PaymentService" |
| "Show me all files with 'helper' in src/utils/" | lexical-code-search: query="path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*helper[^\\/]*$/" |
| "Find error message 'connection timeout'" | lexical-code-search: query="content:\"connection timeout\"" |
| "Where is authenticate function in owner/repo?" | lexical-code-search: query="symbol:authenticate", scopingQuery="repo:owner/repo" |

## Example Workflows

### Example 1: Understanding a Feature
User: "How does payment processing work in owner/myapp?"

1. semantic-code-search: query="How does payment processing work?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="myapp"
   → Discovers: PaymentService, StripeClient, processPayment function
2. lexical-code-search: query="symbol:PaymentService", scopingQuery="repo:owner/myapp"
   → Finds exact class definition location
3. Read the file to understand implementation details

### Example 2: Finding Specific Code
User: "Where is the authenticate function defined?"

1. lexical-code-search: query="symbol:authenticate"
   → Finds exact definition immediately

### Example 3: Debugging from Error Message
User: "I'm seeing 'database connection timeout' error, where does this come from?"

1. lexical-code-search: query="content:\"database connection timeout\""
   → Finds where error message is defined
2. Read surrounding code for context

### Example 4: Exploring a Directory
User: "What helper utilities are in src/utils?"

1. lexical-code-search: query="path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*helper[^\\/]*$/"
   → Lists all files with "helper" in that directory

## Remember

- **semantic-code-search** = Understanding concepts and architecture (requires full sentences, repoOwner, repoName)
- **lexical-code-search** = Finding exact symbols, patterns, and text (use qualifiers: symbol:, content:, path:)
- Use them together: semantic for exploration, lexical for precision

| "How does authentication work in owner/repo?" | semantic-code-search: query="How does authentication work?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="repo" |
| "Where is rate limiting implemented?" | semantic-code-search: query="Where is rate limiting implemented?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="repo" |
| "How are database errors handled?" | semantic-code-search: query="How are database connection errors handled?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="repo" |

### lexical-code-search
**What it does:** Performs exact pattern matching using qualifiers to find specific code elements.

**When to use:**
- User mentions specific symbols, functions, or class names
- Looking for EXACT text matches (error messages, specific strings)
- Need to find files by path or name patterns
- Searching across multiple repositories or organizations
- User provides EXACT code snippets to find
- Following up on semantic search to find exact definitions

**Key qualifiers:**
| Qualifier | Purpose | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| symbol: | Find function/class/method definitions | symbol:AuthService |
| content: | Match exact text in files | content:"error code 500" |
| path: | Match file paths (use regex) | path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*helper[^\\/]*$/ |
| repo: | Scope to specific repository | repo:owner/name |
| org: | Scope to organization | org:github |
| language: | Filter by programming language | language:python |
| is: | Filter by properties | is:archived, is:fork, is:vendored, is:generated |

**Symbol construction:**

Use symbol: when users ask about function, class, or method definitions.

Common patterns:
| User asks | Interpretation | Tool call |
|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| "Where is class Helper defined in monalisa/net?" | Looking for class definition | query="symbol:Helper", scopingQuery="repo:monalisa/net" |
| "What functions are in Foo.go?" | Looking for symbols in file | query="symbol:Foo" |
| "Describe the method MyFunc" | Looking for method definition | query="symbol:MyFunc" |
| "Find AuthService class" | Looking for class definition | query="symbol:AuthService" |
| "Where is authenticate function?" | Looking for function definition | query="symbol:authenticate" |

**Boolean operators:** Use OR, NOT, AND to combine conditions
- Example: symbol:authenticate NOT is:archived

**Parameters:**
- query: The search query with qualifiers
- scopingQuery: (optional) Additional scoping like repo:, org:, path:, language:

**Constructing path queries:**

When users ask for files in specific directories or with certain names, construct regex paths.

Common patterns:
- Any file with "helper" anywhere: path:/.*helper[^\\/]*$/
- Specific directory, any file: path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*$/
- File with extension: path:/.*\\.test\\.js$/

**Examples:**
| User question | Tool call |
|--------------|-----------|
| "Find the PaymentService class definition" | lexical-code-search: query="symbol:PaymentService" |
| "Show me all files with 'helper' in src/utils/" | lexical-code-search: query="path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*helper[^\\/]*$/" |
| "Find error message 'connection timeout'" | lexical-code-search: query="content:\"connection timeout\"" |
| "Where is authenticate function in owner/repo?" | lexical-code-search: query="symbol:authenticate", scopingQuery="repo:owner/repo" |

## Example Workflows

### Example 1: Understanding a Feature
User: "How does payment processing work in owner/myapp?"

1. semantic-code-search: query="How does payment processing work?", repoOwner="owner", repoName="myapp"
   → Discovers: PaymentService, StripeClient, processPayment function
2. lexical-code-search: query="symbol:PaymentService", scopingQuery="repo:owner/myapp"
   → Finds exact class definition location
3. Read the file to understand implementation details

### Example 2: Finding Specific Code
User: "Where is the authenticate function defined?"

1. lexical-code-search: query="symbol:authenticate"
   → Finds exact definition immediately

### Example 3: Debugging from Error Message
User: "I'm seeing 'database connection timeout' error, where does this come from?"

1. lexical-code-search: query="content:\"database connection timeout\""
   → Finds where error message is defined
2. Read surrounding code for context

### Example 4: Exploring a Directory
User: "What helper utilities are in src/utils?"

1. lexical-code-search: query="path:/^src\\/utils\\/[^\\/]*helper[^\\/]*$/"
   → Lists all files with "helper" in that directory

## Remember

- **semantic-code-search** = Understanding concepts and architecture (requires full sentences, repoOwner, repoName)
- **lexical-code-search** = Finding exact symbols, patterns, and text (use qualifiers: symbol:, content:, path:)
- Use them together: semantic for exploration, lexical for precision

"""
🎮 LIFE SKILLS QUEST - Advanced Interactive Game System
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Age-Adaptive Game Engine with Gamification & Real-Time Progression
60-Minute Interactive Module | 5 Skill Levels | XP-Based Rewards

import json

from enum import Enum

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 🎯 ENUMS & DATA CLASSES

class AgeGroup(Enum):
    """Age-based difficulty tiers"""
    YOUNG_EXPLORERS = (5, 9)      # 5-9: Movement, drawing, simple
    JUNIOR_ADVENTURERS = (10, 13) # 10-13: Reasoning, light reflection
    TEEN_CHAMPIONS = (14, 18)     # 14-18: Deep analysis, strategy
    ADULT_MASTERS = (18, 100)     # 18+: Advanced psychology


class SkillLevel(Enum):
    """Game progression levels"""
    LEVEL_1_AWARENESS = "Self-Awareness & Emotions"
    LEVEL_2_COMMUNICATION = "Communication & Empathy"
    LEVEL_3_DECISION = "Decision-Making & Problem-Solving"
    LEVEL_4_CREATIVITY = "Creative & Critical Thinking"
    LEVEL_5_WELLNESS = "Stress & Healthy Relationships"


class ActivityType(Enum):
    """Different activity game mechanics"""
    DRAWING = "Drawing Activity"
    CARD_GAME = "Card Game"
    SCENARIO = "Scenario Challenge"
    PUZZLE = "Puzzle Solving"
    REFLECTION = "Reflection Exercise"
    SPEED_CHALLENGE = "Speed Challenge"
    DISCUSSION = "Group Discussion"


@dataclass
class StudentProfile:
    """Player/Student data structure"""
    name: str
    age: int
    xp_points: int = 0
    level_progress: Dict[str, int] = None
    badges: List[str] = None
    activity_history: List[Dict] = None
    current_level: int = 1
    streak_days: int = 0
    last_played: str = ""
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        if self.level_progress is None:
            self.level_progress = {level.name: 0 for level in SkillLevel}
        if self.badges is None:
            self.badges = []
        if self.activity_history is None:
            self.activity_history = []


@dataclass
class GameActivity:
    """Individual activity structure"""
    level: SkillLevel
    activity_type: ActivityType
    title: str
    description: str
    xp_reward: int
    age_variant: Dict[str, dict]  # age_group: {content, difficulty, variants}
    teaching_point: str
    psychology_theory: str


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 🧠 AGE-ADAPTIVE ENGINE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class AgeAdaptiveEngine:
    """Dynamically adjust difficulty, language, and game mechanics by age"""
    
    def __init__(self, age: int):
        self.age = age
        self.age_group = self._determine_age_group(age)
    
    def _determine_age_group(self, age: int) -> AgeGroup:
        """Classify age into learning tier"""
        for group in AgeGroup:
            min_age, max_age = group.value
            if min_age <= age <= max_age:
                return group
        return AgeGroup.ADULT_MASTERS
    
    def get_language_complexity(self) -> str:
        """Adjust vocabulary and sentence length"""
        complexity_map = {
            AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS: "simple",
            AgeGroup.JUNIOR_ADVENTURERS: "intermediate",
            AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS: "advanced",
            AgeGroup.ADULT_MASTERS: "technical"
        }
        return complexity_map[self.age_group]
    
    def get_activity_duration(self, base_duration: int) -> int:
        """Adjust activity length - younger = shorter attention span"""
        duration_multiplier = {
            AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS: 0.7,
            AgeGroup.JUNIOR_ADVENTURERS: 0.9,
            AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS: 1.0,
            AgeGroup.ADULT_MASTERS: 1.2
        }
        return int(base_duration * duration_multiplier[self.age_group])
    
    def get_game_mechanics(self) -> Dict:
        """Return age-appropriate game mechanics"""
        mechanics = {
            AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS: {
                "use_emojis": True,
                "require_drawing": True,
                "time_pressure": False,
                "narrative_style": "storytelling",
                "reward_frequency": "high",
                "visual_feedback": True,
                "movement_breaks": True
            },
            AgeGroup.JUNIOR_ADVENTURERS: {
                "use_emojis": True,
                "require_drawing": False,
                "time_pressure": "gentle",
                "narrative_style": "quest_based",
                "reward_frequency": "medium",
                "visual_feedback": True,
                "leaderboard": True
            },
            AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS: {
                "use_emojis": False,
                "require_drawing": False,
                "time_pressure": "moderate",
                "narrative_style": "challenge_based",
                "reward_frequency": "performance_based",
                "analytics": True,
                "competitive_mode": True
            },
            AgeGroup.ADULT_MASTERS: {
                "use_emojis": False,
                "detailed_theory": True,
                "time_pressure": "strict",
                "narrative_style": "science_based",
                "reward_frequency": "achievement_based",
                "advanced_analytics": True,
                "certification_path": True
            }
        }
        return mechanics[self.age_group]


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 🎮 ACTIVITY SYSTEM
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class ActivityBase(ABC):
    """Abstract base class for all activities"""
    
    def __init__(self, student: StudentProfile, adaptive_engine: AgeAdaptiveEngine):
        self.student = student
        self.adaptive_engine = adaptive_engine
        self.start_time = None
        self.end_time = None
        self.score = 0
    
    @abstractmethod
    def play(self):
        """Execute the activity"""
        pass
    
    def display_with_emoji(self, text: str, emoji: str = "🎮"):
        """Display text with emoji if age-appropriate"""
        if self.adaptive_engine.get_game_mechanics().get("use_emojis"):
            print(f"{emoji} {text}")
        else:
            print(text)
    
    def record_activity(self, activity_type: str, result: Dict):
        """Log activity for progress tracking"""
        self.student.activity_history.append({
            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
            "activity": activity_type,
            "level": result.get("level"),
            "xp_earned": result.get("xp_earned"),
            "performance": result.get("performance")
        })

    """LEVEL 1: Self-Awareness - Shield Drawing/Writing"""
    
    def play(self):
        self.display_with_emoji("\n🛡️  STRENGTH SHIELD - Know Yourself Better!", "🛡️")
        print("━" * 50)

        if self.adaptive_engine.age_group == AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS:

        else:
            self._play_older_students(mechanics)

        print("  2️⃣  One talent (what can you do well?)")

class EmotionCardsGame(ActivityBase):
    """LEVEL 1: Emotion recognition & healthy coping"""
    
    EMOTIONS_DATABASE = {
        "😊": {"emotion": "Happy", "coping": ["Smile at others", "Share joy"]},
        "😢": {"emotion": "Sad", "coping": ["Talk to someone", "Journal feelings"]},
        "😡": {"emotion": "Angry", "coping": ["Deep breathing", "Take a break"]},
        "😨": {"emotion": "Scared", "coping": ["Tell a trusted adult", "Deep breathing"]},
        "😔": {"emotion": "Disappointed", "coping": ["Try again", "Celebrate small wins"]},
        "😌": {"emotion": "Calm", "coping": ["Meditation", "Quiet time"]},
        "🤗": {"emotion": "Grateful", "coping": ["Say thank you", "Help someone"]},
        "😤": {"emotion": "Frustrated", "coping": ["Take a walk", "Ask for help"]}
    }
    
    def play(self):
        self.display_with_emoji("\n💬 EMOTION CARDS - Feelings & Coping Skills", "💬")
        print("━" * 50)
        
        difficulty = self._get_difficulty()
        num_cards = difficulty
        cards_to_play = random.sample(list(self.EMOTIONS_DATABASE.items()), num_cards)
        
        correct_answers = 0
        
        for emoji, data in cards_to_play:
            print(f"\nWhat emotion is this? {emoji}")
            player_answer = input("Your answer: ").lower()
            
            if player_answer == data["emotion"].lower():
                print(f"✅ Correct! It's {data['emotion']}")
                correct_answers += 1
            else:
                print(f"❌ It's {data['emotion']}, but you'll get it next time!")
            
            print(f"💡 Healthy ways to handle {data['emotion']}:")
            for coping in data["coping"]:
                print(f"   • {coping}")
        
        accuracy = (correct_answers / len(cards_to_play)) * 100
        xp_earned = int(10 * (accuracy / 100) + 5)
        
        self.student.xp_points += xp_earned
        self.student.level_progress["LEVEL_1_AWARENESS"] += xp_earned
        
        print(f"\n🎯 Accuracy: {accuracy:.0f}% | +{xp_earned} Emotion XP!")
    
    def _get_difficulty(self) -> int:
        """Return number of emotion cards based on age"""
        difficulty_map = {
            AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS: 3,
            AgeGroup.JUNIOR_ADVENTURERS: 5,
            AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS: 7,
            AgeGroup.ADULT_MASTERS: 8
        }
        return difficulty_map[self.adaptive_engine.age_group]

class ScenarioChallenge(ActivityBase):
    """LEVEL 3: Decision-Making with STOP method"""
    
    SCENARIOS = {
        AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS: [
            {
                "situation": "Your friend wants you to run away from a scary activity",
                "options": ["A) Run away", "B) Try it with your friend"],
                "consequences": ["You feel scared alone", "You discover it's fun!"]
            }
        ],
        AgeGroup.JUNIOR_ADVENTURERS: [
            {
                "situation": "Your friend asks you to skip homework and play games",
                "options": ["A) Say yes and skip", "B) Do homework first, then play"],
                "consequences": [
                    "Grades drop, parents angry",
                    "You feel responsible and can enjoy games guilt-free"
                ]
            }
        ],
        AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS: [
            {
                "situation": "Friends pressure you to do something you're not comfortable with",
                "options": [
                    "A) Say yes to fit in",
                    "B) Politely decline and suggest alternative",
                    "C) Leave the group"
                ],
                "consequences": [

                ]
            }
        ]
    }
    
    def play(self):
        self.display_with_emoji("\n🧠 SCENARIO CHALLENGE - STOP Method", "����")
        print("━" * 50)
        
        print("\n📋 Using the STOP Method:")
        print("  S - STOP (pause, don't react immediately)")
        print("  T - THINK (what are my options?)")
        print("  O - OPTIONS (list possibilities)")
        print("  P - PICK (choose the best one)")
        
        # Get appropriate scenario
        scenarios = self.SCENARIOS.get(self.adaptive_engine.age_group, self.SCENARIOS[AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS])
        scenario = random.choice(scenarios)

class CriticalThinkingGame(ActivityBase):
    """LEVEL 4: Real or Fake - Fact checking"""
    
    STATEMENTS = {
        AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS: [
            {"statement": "Cats can see in the dark", "answer": "true", "explanation": "Cats have special eyes for night vision"},
            {"statement": "Fish can climb trees", "answer": "false", "explanation": "Fish live in water, not trees"}
        ],
        AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS: [
            {"statement": "The human brain uses 10% of its capacity", "answer": "false", "explanation": "Myth! We use nearly 100% of our brain"},
            {"statement": "Social media increases anxiety in teenagers", "answer": "true", "explanation": "Research shows correlation with excessive use"}
        ]
    }
    
    def play(self):
        self.display_with_emoji("\n🔍 CRITICAL THINKING - Real or Fake?", "🔍")
        print("━" * 50)
        
        statements = self.STATEMENTS.get(
            self.adaptive_engine.age_group, 
            self.STATEMENTS[AgeGroup.TEEN_CHAMPIONS]
        )
        
        correct = 0
        for stmt in statements:
            print(f"\n❓ {stmt['statement']}")
            answer = input("Real (T) or Fake (F)? ").upper()
            
            correct_answer = "T" if stmt['answer'] == "true" else "F"
            if answer == correct_answer:
                print(f"✅ Correct!")
                correct += 1
            else:
                print(f"❌ Actually, {stmt['answer'].upper()}")
            
            print(f"💡 Why: {stmt['explanation']}")
        
        accuracy = (correct / len(statements)) * 100
        xp = int(15 * (accuracy / 100) + 5)
        self.student.xp_points += xp
        self.student.level_progress["LEVEL_4_CREATIVITY"] += xp
        print(f"\n🎉 Accuracy: {accuracy:.0f}% | +{xp} Critical Thinking XP!")

    """LEVEL 5: Stress management - Guided breathing"""
    
    def play(self):
        self.display_with_emoji("\n🧘 CALM MODE - Breathing Exercise", "🧘")
        print("━" * 50)
        
        if self.adaptive_engine.age_group == AgeGroup.YOUNG_EXPLORERS:
            self._child_breathing()
        else:
            self._advanced_breathing()
    
    def _child_breathing(self):
        print("\n🎈 Let's blow up a big balloon with our breath!")
        print("Breathe in... 1... 2... 3... (slow)\n")
        time.sleep(3)
        
        print("Hold it... 1... 2... (steady)\n")
        time.sleep(2)
        
        print("Breathe out... 1... 2... 3... 4... (slow)\n")
        time.sleep(4)
        
        print("✨ Repeat 5 times!\n")
        for i in range(5):
            print(f"Round {i+1}: In... Hold... Out...")
            time.sleep(3)
        
        print("\n💚 How does your body feel now?")
        feeling = input("Tell me: ")

        self.student.xp_points += 10

    def _advanced_breathing(self):

class GameSession:
    """Main game orchestrator"""
    
    def __init__(self, student: StudentProfile):
        self.student = student
        self.adaptive_engine = AgeAdaptiveEngine(student.age)
        self.start_time = datetime.now()
        self.activities_played = []
        self.total_duration = 0
    
    def display_header(self):
        """Show game intro"""
        print("\n" + "="*60)
        print("🎮 LIFE SKILLS QUEST - Advanced Interactive Module")
        print("="*60)
        print(f"👋 Welcome, {self.student.name}! (Age {self.student.age})")
        print(f"📊 Current XP: {self.student.xp_points} | Level: {self.student.current_level}")
        print(f"🏆 Badges: {len(self.student.badges)}")
        print("="*60 + "\n")
    
    def display_level_menu(self):
        """Show available levels"""
        print("\n🗺️  QUEST MAP - Select Your Level:\n")
        
        levels = [
            ("1", "🛡️  Self-Awareness & Emotions"),
            ("2", "💬 Communication & Empathy"),
            ("3", "🧠 Decision-Making"),
            ("4", "💡 Creative & Critical Thinking"),
            ("5", "🌿 Stress & Wellness (BOSS LEVEL)")
        ]
        
        for code, name in levels:
            progress = self.student.level_progress.get(f"LEVEL_{code}_" + ("AWARENESS" if code == "1" else "COMMUNICATION" if code == "2" else "DECISION" if code == "3" else "CREATIVITY" if code == "4" else "WELLNESS"), 0)
            xp_display = f" [{progress} XP]" if progress > 0 else ""
            print(f"  {code}. {name}{xp_display}")
        
        print(f"  Q. Quit & Save")
        return input("\nChoose (1-5 or Q): ").strip().upper()
    
    def play_level(self, level_choice: str):
        """Execute activities for chosen level"""
        
        level_activities = {
            "1": [
                StrengthShieldActivity(self.student, self.adaptive_engine),
                EmotionCardsGame(self.student, self.adaptive_engine)
            ],
            "2": [
                CommunicationChallenge(self.student, self.adaptive_engine)
            ],
            "3": [
                ScenarioChallenge(self.student, self.adaptive_engine)
            ],
            "4": [
                CreativityChallenge(self.student, self.adaptive_engine),
                CriticalThinkingGame(self.student, self.adaptive_engine)
            ],
            "5": [
                BreathingExercise(self.student, self.adaptive_engine),
                KindnessChain(self.student, self.adaptive_engine)
            ]
        }
        
        activities = level_activities.get(level_choice, [])
        
        for activity in activities:
            activity.play()
            self.activities_played.append(type(activity).__name__)
            time.sleep(1)
    
    def show_progress(self):
        """Display current progress"""
        print("\n" + "="*60)
        print("📊 PROGRESS DASHBOARD")
        print("="*60)
        
        total_xp = self.student.xp_points
        print(f"\n🏆 Total XP: {total_xp}")
        
        print("\n📈 Level Progress:")
        for level in SkillLevel:
            xp = self.student.level_progress.get(level.name, 0)
            bar_length = xp // 5
            bar = "█" * bar_length + "░" * (20 - bar_length)
            print(f"  {level.name[:30]:<30} {bar} {xp} XP")
        
        print(f"\n🏅 Badges Earned: {', '.join(self.student.badges) if self.student.badges else 'None yet'}")
        
        # Determine title
        title = self._get_title()
        print(f"\n⭐ Your Title: {title}")
    
    def _get_title(self) -> str:
        """Assign title based on XP"""
        if self.student.xp_points < 50:
            return "🌱 Skill Explorer"
        elif self.student.xp_points < 150:
            return "🚀 Skill Adventurer"
        elif self.student.xp_points < 300:
            return "🏆 Life Skills Champion"
        else:
            return "👑 Master of Life Skills"
    
    def save_progress(self):
        """Save student profile to file"""
        filename = f"student_{self.student.name.replace(' ', '_')}.json"
        with open(filename, 'w') as f:
            data = asdict(self.student)
            json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
        print(f"\n💾 Progress saved to {filename}")
    
    def run(self):
        """Main game loop"""
        self.display_header()
        
        while True:
            choice = self.display_level_menu()
            
            if choice == "Q":
                self.show_progress()
                self.save_progress()
                print("\n🎮 Thanks for playing! See you next quest! 👋")
                break
            
            elif choice in ["1", "2", "3", "4", "5"]:
                self.play_level(choice)
            
            else:
                print("❌ Invalid choice. Try again!")

class LeaderboardSystem:

    def display_leaderboard(self):
        """Show top performers"""
        print("\n" + "="*60)
        print("🏆 GLOBAL LEADERBOARD")
        print("="*60 + "\n")
        
        sorted_students = sorted(self.students, key=lambda s: s.xp_points, reverse=True)
        
        for rank, student in enumerate(sorted_students[:10], 1):
            medal = "🥇" if rank == 1 else "🥈" if rank == 2 else "🥉"
            print(f"{medal} #{rank} {student.name} - {student.xp_points} XP")


class AchievementSystem:
    """Badge and achievement unlocking"""
    
    ACHIEVEMENTS = {
        "first_win": {"name": "First Steps", "desc": "Complete your first activity", "emoji": "🚀"},
        "perfect_emotion": {"name": "Emotion Master", "desc": "Get 100% on Emotion Cards", "emoji": "😊"},
        "creativity_pro": {"name": "Creative Genius", "desc": "Get bonus XP in Creativity", "emoji": "💡"},
        "kindness_streak": {"name": "Kindness Champion", "desc": "Show kindness daily", "emoji": "💝"},
        "stress_warrior": {"name": "Zen Master", "desc": "Reduce stress by 5+ points", "emoji": "🧘"}
    }
    
    @staticmethod
    def unlock_achievement(student: StudentProfile, achievement_id: str):
        """Add achievement to student"""
        if achievement_id in AchievementSystem.ACHIEVEMENTS:
            achievement = AchievementSystem.ACHIEVEMENTS[achievement_id]
            if achievement["name"] not in student.badges:
                student.badges.append(achievement["name"])
                print(f"\n🏆 ACHIEVEMENT UNLOCKED: {achievement['emoji']} {achievement['name']}")
                print(f"   {achievement['desc']}")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 📱 USER INTERFACE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def create_student() -> StudentProfile:
    """Interactive student creation"""
    print("\n" + "="*60)
    print("👋 Welcome to LIFE SKILLS QUEST!")
    print("="*60)
    
    name = input("\n📝 What's your name? ")
    
    while True:
        try:
            age = int(input("🎂 How old are you? "))
            if 5 <= age <= 100:
                break
            else:
                print("❌ Please enter an age between 5 and 100")
        except ValueError:
            print("❌ Please enter a valid number")
    
    return StudentProfile(name=name, age=age)


def load_student() -> Optional[StudentProfile]:
    """Load existing student profile"""
    print("\n💾 Existing Profiles:")
    
    json_files = [f for f in os.listdir('.') if f.startswith('student_') and f.endswith('.json')]
    
    if not json_files:
        print("   No saved profiles found")
        return None
    
    for idx, filename in enumerate(json_files, 1):
        print(f"  {idx}. {filename}")
    
    try:
        choice = int(input("\nLoad profile (number) or press 0 to create new: "))
        if 0 < choice <= len(json_files):
            with open(json_files[choice-1], 'r') as f:
                data = json.load(f)
                profile = StudentProfile(**data)
                print(f"\n✅ Welcome back, {profile.name}!")
                return profile
    except:
        pass
    
    return None


def main():
    """Main entry point"""
    print("\n🎮 LIFE SKILLS QUEST - GAME STARTUP")
    
    # Load or create student
    student = load_student()
    if not student:
        student = create_student()
    
    # Start game session
    session = GameSession(student)
    session.run()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

class AnalyticsDashboard:
    """Advanced progress tracking & insights"""
    
    def __init__(self, student):
        self.student = student
    
    def generate_report(self) -> Dict:
        """Create comprehensive learning report"""
        
        total_activities = len(self.student.activity_history)
        avg_performance = sum([
            a.get("xp_earned", 0) for a in self.student.activity_history
        ]) / max(total_activities, 1)
        
        report = {
            "student_name": self.student.name,
            "age": self.student.age,
            "total_xp": self.student.xp_points,
            "activities_completed": total_activities,
            "avg_xp_per_activity": avg_performance,
            "strongest_skill": self._get_strongest_skill(),
            "needs_improvement": self._get_weakest_skill(),
            "streak": self.student.streak_days,
            "badges": self.student.badges,
            "engagement_score": self._calculate_engagement(),
            "learning_velocity": self._calculate_velocity()
        }
        
        return report
    
    def _get_strongest_skill(self) -> str:
        """Find student's best skill"""
        best_skill = max(
            self.student.level_progress.items(),
            key=lambda x: x[1]
        )
        return best_skill[0].replace("_", " ")
    
    def _get_weakest_skill(self) -> str:
        """Identify area needing improvement"""
        weakest = min(
            self.student.level_progress.items(),
            key=lambda x: x[1]
        )
        return weakest[0].replace("_", " ")
    
    def _calculate_engagement(self) -> int:
        """Rate student engagement (0-100)"""
        if not self.student.activity_history:
            return 0
        
        # Engagement = variety of activities attempted
        unique_activities = set([
            a.get("activity") for a in self.student.activity_history
        ])
        
        return min(100, (len(unique_activities) / 8) * 100)
    
    def _calculate_velocity(self) -> str:
        """Learning speed trend"""
        if len(self.student.activity_history) < 2:
            return "Just started! 🚀"
        
        recent_xp = sum([
            a.get("xp_earned", 0) 
            for a in self.student.activity_history[-3:]
        ])
        
        if recent_xp > 40:
            return "🔥 Accelerating!"
        elif recent_xp > 20:
            return "📈 Steady progress"
        else:
            return "🐢 Building momentum"
    
    def print_dashboard(self):
        """Display analytics in terminal"""
        report = self.generate_report()
        
        print("\n" + "="*70)
        print("📊 ADVANCED ANALYTICS DASHBOARD")
        print("="*70)
        
        print(f"\n👤 Student: {report['student_name']} (Age {report['age']})")
        print(f"🎯 Total XP Earned: {report['total_xp']}")
        print(f"📈 Activities Completed: {report['activities_completed']}")
        print(f"⚡ Average XP per Activity: {report['avg_xp_per_activity']:.1f}")
        
        print(f"\n🌟 Strongest Skill: {report['strongest_skill']}")
        print(f"📌 Needs Improvement: {report['needs_improvement']}")
        print(f"🔥 Learning Velocity: {report['learning_velocity']}")
        print(f"📊 Engagement Score: {report['engagement_score']:.0f}%")
        
        print(f"\n🏆 Badges Earned: {len(report['badges'])}")
        for badge in report['badges']:
            print(f"   ✅ {badge}")

"""
🎮 LIFE SKILLS QUEST - Flask REST API
Full-stack backend with PostgreSQL integration
"""

from flask import Flask, jsonify
from flask_cors import CORS
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from flask_migrate import Migrate
from flask_jwt_extended import JWTManager
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os
from datetime import timedelta

# Load environment variables
load_dotenv()

# Initialize extensions
db = SQLAlchemy()
migrate = Migrate()
jwt = JWTManager()

def create_app(config_name='development'):
    """Application factory"""
    app = Flask(__name__)
    
    # Load configuration
    from config import config
    app.config.from_object(config[config_name])
    
    # Initialize extensions
    db.init_app(app)
    migrate.init_app(app, db)
    jwt.init_app(app)
    
    # Enable CORS
    CORS(app, resources={r"/api/*": {"origins": "*"}})
    
    # Register blueprints
    from routes.auth import auth_bp
    from routes.game import game_bp
    from routes.analytics import analytics_bp
    
    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp, url_prefix='/api/auth')
    app.register_blueprint(game_bp, url_prefix='/api/game')
    app.register_blueprint(analytics_bp, url_prefix='/api/analytics')
    
    # Error handlers
    @app.errorhandler(404)
    def not_found(error):
        return jsonify({"error": "Resource not found"}), 404
    
    @app.errorhandler(500)
    def server_error(error):
        return jsonify({"error": "Internal server error"}), 500
    
    # Health check
    @app.route('/api/health')
    def health():
        return jsonify({"status": "healthy", "version": "1.0.0"}), 200
    
    # Create tables
    with app.app_context():
        db.create_all()
    
    return app

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app = create_app(os.getenv('FLASK_ENV', 'development'))
    app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000)

"""
User/Student model
"""

from app import db
from werkzeug.security import generate_password_hash, check_password_hash
from datetime import datetime

class User(db.Model):
    """Student/User profile"""
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    email = db.Column(db.String(120), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    password_hash = db.Column(db.String(255), nullable=False)
    first_name = db.Column(db.String(50))
    last_name = db.Column(db.String(50))
    age = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)
    age_group = db.Column(db.String(30))  # YOUNG_EXPLORERS, JUNIOR_ADVENTURERS, etc
    profile_picture = db.Column(db.String(255))
    bio = db.Column(db.Text)
    
    # Game stats
    total_xp = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    current_level = db.Column(db.Integer, default=1)
    current_title = db.Column(db.String(100), default="Skill Explorer")
    streak_days = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    last_played = db.Column(db.DateTime)
    
    # Preferences
    language = db.Column(db.String(10), default='en')
    difficulty_preference = db.Column(db.String(20), default='adaptive')
    
    # Account info
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    updated_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow)
    is_active = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=True)
    
    # Relationships
    progress = db.relationship('StudentProgress', backref='student', lazy=True, cascade='all, delete-orphan')
    activities = db.relationship('ActivityProgress', backref='student', lazy=True, cascade='all, delete-orphan')
    achievements = db.relationship('Achievement', backref='student', lazy=True, cascade='all, delete-orphan')
    
    def set_password(self, password):
        """Hash password"""
        self.password_hash = generate_password_hash(password)
    
    def check_password(self, password):
        """Verify password"""
        return check_password_hash(self.password_hash, password)
    
    def to_dict(self):
        """Convert to dictionary"""
        return {
            'id': self.id,
            'username': self.username,
            'email': self.email,
            'first_name': self.first_name,
            'last_name': self.last_name,
            'age': self.age,
            'age_group': self.age_group,
            'profile_picture': self.profile_picture,
            'total_xp': self.total_xp,
            'current_level': self.current_level,
            'current_title': self.current_title,
            'created_at': self.created_at.isoformat() if self.created_at else None
        }
    
    def __repr__(self):
        return f'<User {self.username}>'

"""
Activity and progress models
"""

from app import db
from datetime import datetime
import json

class Activity(db.Model):
    """Activity definitions"""
    __tablename__ = 'activities'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False)
    description = db.Column(db.Text)
    level = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)  # 1-5
    skill_type = db.Column(db.String(50))  # AWARENESS, COMMUNICATION, etc
    activity_type = db.Column(db.String(50))  # DRAWING, CARD_GAME, SCENARIO, etc
    
    # Content by age group
    content_young = db.Column(db.JSON)  # 5-9 age version
    content_junior = db.Column(db.JSON)  # 10-13 age version
    content_teen = db.Column(db.JSON)    # 14-18 age version
    content_adult = db.Column(db.JSON)   # 18+ age version
    
    xp_reward = db.Column(db.Integer, default=10)
    duration_minutes = db.Column(db.Integer, default=5)
    psychology_theory = db.Column(db.String(200))
    teaching_point = db.Column(db.Text)
    
    is_active = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=True)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    
    # Relationships
    progress_records = db.relationship('ActivityProgress', backref='activity', lazy=True, cascade='all, delete-orphan')
    
    def get_content_for_age(self, age: int) -> dict:
        """Get age-appropriate content"""
        if age <= 9:
            return self.content_young or {}
        elif age <= 13:
            return self.content_junior or {}
        elif age <= 18:
            return self.content_teen or {}
        else:
            return self.content_adult or {}
    
    def to_dict(self, age=None):
        """Convert to dictionary"""
        data = {
            'id': self.id,
            'title': self.title,
            'description': self.description,
            'level': self.level,
            'skill_type': self.skill_type,
            'activity_type': self.activity_type,
            'xp_reward': self.xp_reward,
            'duration_minutes': self.duration_minutes,
            'teaching_point': self.teaching_point
        }
        
        if age:
            data['content'] = self.get_content_for_age(age)
        
        return data


class ActivityProgress(db.Model):
    """Track individual activity completion"""
    __tablename__ = 'activity_progress'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False, index=True)
    activity_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('activities.id'), nullable=False)
    
    xp_earned = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    score = db.Column(db.Float, default=0.0)
    accuracy = db.Column(db.Float, default=0.0)  # 0-100%
    time_spent_seconds = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    
    responses = db.Column(db.JSON)  # Store user responses
    feedback = db.Column(db.Text)
    
    completed_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    is_completed = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=True)
    
    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'id': self.id,
            'user_id': self.user_id,
            'activity_id': self.activity_id,
            'xp_earned': self.xp_earned,
            'score': self.score,
            'accuracy': self.accuracy,
            'time_spent_seconds': self.time_spent_seconds,
            'completed_at': self.completed_at.isoformat() if self.completed_at else None

"""
Student progress and achievement models
"""

from app import db
from datetime import datetime

class StudentProgress(db.Model):
    """Overall progress by skill level"""
    __tablename__ = 'student_progress'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), unique=True, nullable=False)
    
    # XP by level
    level1_awareness_xp = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    level2_communication_xp = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    level3_decision_xp = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    level4_creativity_xp = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    level5_wellness_xp = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    
    # Completion
    total_activities_completed = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
    average_score = db.Column(db.Float, default=0.0)
    
    last_updated = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow)
    
    def get_all_xp(self) -> dict:
        return {
            'self_awareness': self.level1_awareness_xp,
            'communication': self.level2_communication_xp,
            'decision_making': self.level3_decision_xp,
            'creativity': self.level4_creativity_xp,
            'wellness': self.level5_wellness_xp
        }
    
    def total_xp(self) -> int:
        return sum(self.get_all_xp().values())
    
    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'user_id': self.user_id,
            'xp_by_level': self.get_all_xp(),
            'total_xp': self.total_xp(),
            'total_activities': self.total_activities_completed,
            'average_score': self.average_score
        }


class Achievement(db.Model):
    """Achievements/badges earned by students"""
    __tablename__ = 'achievements'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False, index=True)

    badge_name = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
    badge_emoji = db.Column(db.String(10))

    earned_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

        return {
            'badge_id': self.badge_id,

            'badge_emoji': self.badge_emoji,
            'description': self.description,
